openclawCommands.title: OpenClaw Command Reference
openclawCommands.subtitle: Common commands curated from the official CLI reference. Copy any command or open its exact official page or section.
openclawCommands.verifiedSource: Content and links verified against official docs
openclawCommands.copyFailedBody: Could not write to the clipboard. Please try again.
openclawCommands.openOfficialDocs: Open the matching official documentation section

items.setup: Run first-time setup; --baseline creates only the baseline config and workspace without the full guided flow.
items.configGet: Read the redacted configuration snapshot; secrets are never printed.
items.configSet: Preview a path-based configuration write; remove --dry-run only after reviewing it. Values parse as JSON5 when possible.
items.configValidate: Validate the current configuration against its active schema without starting the Gateway.
items.updateStatus: Show the active update channel, version source, and update availability.
items.gatewayInstall: Install and register the managed Gateway service, writing a launchd, systemd, or schtasks service definition for the current platform.
items.gatewayStart: Start the installed Gateway background service; run gateway install first if the service has not been registered.
items.gatewayStop: Stop the managed Gateway service. On macOS this lasts for the current boot by default; use --disable to keep it disabled.
items.gatewaySafeRestart: Check active work and wait for it to drain before one coalesced restart; force restart after the configured timeout.
items.gatewayRunVerbose: Run the Gateway in the foreground with detailed startup output in the current terminal, useful for diagnosing service startup and configuration failures.
items.gatewayStatus: Check the service manager, connection, and read-only RPC. --require-rpc exits nonzero when RPC is unavailable and does not create an identity or pairing.
items.gatewayProbe: Probe both the configured remote endpoint and loopback, reporting reachability, authentication, and read-only RPC separately to isolate connection failures.
items.gatewayStability: Read the latest persisted stability record, even after the Gateway crashes or fails to start.
items.gatewayDiagnosticsExport: Create a redacted support ZIP containing configuration, status, health, logs, and stability records at the requested output path.
items.statusAll: Run the complete read-only status report, including the security audit, plugin compatibility, and memory/vector capability status.
items.statusDeep: Run live probes for WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal, and other channels, plus the security audit.
items.healthVerbose: Fetch health over WebSocket RPC and force live health probes for every account and agent.
items.logsFollow: Tail Gateway logs over RPC in local time, reconnecting automatically with exponential backoff after interruptions.
items.doctorLint: Emit structured JSON diagnostics without prompts, repairs, or file writes; suitable for CI and read-only troubleshooting.
items.doctorFix: Apply supported guided repairs across the Gateway, channels, plugins, skills, models, state, and migrations.
items.securityAuditDeep: Add live Gateway probes and plugin collectors to cold configuration and filesystem checks; the entire audit remains read-only.
items.secretsAudit: Check plaintext secrets, unresolved SecretRefs, auth precedence drift, and legacy residue; exits 1 for findings and 2 for unresolved references.
items.secretsReload: Re-resolve SecretRefs and atomically replace the runtime snapshot only when all succeed; failures retain the last known good snapshot and never write config.
items.modelsStatus: Show the resolved default, fallbacks, and auth overview, plus usage and quota snapshots when supported. Without --probe it makes no real model request.
items.modelsStatusCheck: Use as an auth health gate: missing or expired exits 1, expiring soon exits 2, and healthy exits 0; it never repairs configuration.
items.modelsStatusProbe: Make a real model request to one provider. It may use tokens, hit rate limits, or incur charges, and distinguishes auth, rate-limit, billing, and timeout failures.
items.modelsListProvider: Read the full catalog for an exact provider ID. Unauthenticated static entries may appear unavailable; this writes no models.json and does not prove callability.
items.modelsListLocal: Show local models only, reading existing configuration and catalog state without rewriting models.json.
items.modelsSet: Write a provider/model or alias to global agents.defaults.model.primary. It rejects --agent and may repair the Codex or Copilot runtime plugin.
items.modelsSetImage: Write the image model to global agents.defaults.imageModel.primary. It rejects --agent and does not repair runtime plugins.
items.modelsAliasesList: List the JSON mapping from model aliases to canonical provider/model identifiers.
items.modelsAliasesAdd: Add or update an alias after resolving its target to a canonical model key; updating an alias repoints it instead of creating an alias chain.
items.modelsAliasesRemove: Remove a configured alias without deleting a model. Built-in aliases absent from config cannot be removed, only repointed.
items.modelsFallbacksList: Show the text-model fallback order from agents.defaults.model.fallbacks as JSON.
items.modelsFallbacksAdd: Add a canonical model or configured alias to text-model fallbacks; the same group also supports remove and clear.
items.modelsImageFallbacksAdd: Add a model to the separate image-model fallback list; list, remove, and clear are also supported.
items.modelsAuthList: List saved auth profiles for a provider; the official command never prints tokens, API keys, or OAuth secrets.
items.modelsAuthAdd: Start the interactive auth helper, enter the provider's OAuth, API-key, or manual-token flow, and write the resulting auth configuration.
items.modelsAuthLogin: Run an installed provider plugin's auth flow and apply its recommended default. openai defaults to ChatGPT/Codex; API keys require --method api-key.
items.modelsAuthPasteApiKey: Read an API key from the terminal prompt and default to <provider>:manual. Never place the key in arguments; automation should provide it over stdin.
items.modelsAuthOrderGet: Read the current agent's auth-profile order override for a provider from auth-state.json, which is separate from auth.order configuration.
items.modelsAuthOrderSet: Validate profile IDs and set their priority in argument order. Omitted profiles are not probed and report excluded_by_auth_order.
items.modelsAuthOrderClear: Clear the current agent's auth-order override and restore configured ordering or round robin without deleting any auth profiles.
items.channelsList: List channel accounts with installed, configured, and enabled states; --all also shows unconfigured or installable channels.
items.channelsProbe: Run live probes and available audits for channel accounts; fall back to configuration summaries if the Gateway is unreachable.
items.channelsAdd: Add a channel account; run without flags to enter the interactive setup wizard.
items.channelsLogin: Run interactive login for a supported channel from a terminal on the Gateway host.
items.agentsList: List isolated agents; --bindings includes their complete channel routing rules.
items.sessions: List persisted sessions filtered by recent activity; session rows are not a channel liveness check.
items.messageSend: Send one message to a supported external channel; target formats vary by channel.
items.cronList: List Gateway scheduled jobs with their resolved delivery targets.
items.cronCreate: Create a Gateway scheduled job from a cron expression and prompt.
items.cronRun: Queue a manual run for one job; --wait blocks until that run reaches a terminal state.
items.skillsList: List skills visible to the current workspace; use --eligible to show only skills that are ready to run.
items.skillsCheck: Check whether each skill's required binaries, environment variables, and configuration are ready.

skills.hubInstallDoneHint: Active on next conversation — no restart needed

skills.skillshubCliInstallFailWin: Auto-install failed. Copy the command and run it in PowerShell.
skills.skillshubCliHint: Skills install faster from China via Tencent CDN. Optional — skills still work without it.
skills.skillshubCliRequired: SkillHub CLI is required to install skills. Click to auto-install.
skills.skillshubFallbackNote: SkillHub API is unreachable — showing ClawHub catalog (same content). Installation will still use Tencent CDN when available.
skills.skillshubApiError: SkillHub is only accessible from mainland China networks. Browse the website directly or search ClawHub above.

voiceWake.hint: System speech recognition transcribes directly where supported. Other desktop environments use local VAD to capture audio and send it to OpenClaw. Speaking interrupts the current reply. Picovoice settings are reserved for a future wake-word adapter.

voiceWake.notConfigured: Using system speech recognition or local VAD

maintenance.repairConfirmMessage: This runs the official OpenClaw repair flow. Configuration may change and will be scanned again when it finishes.
maintenance.repairFailedMessage: The official repair command did not complete successfully. Use the new scan results to resolve the remaining issues.
maintenance.noFindings: Configuration, plugins, and Doctor checks passed

offline.title: Not Connected
offline.description: Unable to connect to OpenClaw Gateway. The app will reconnect automatically when the gateway becomes available.
offline.connectingDescription: OpenClaw Gateway is starting and the app is establishing a connection automatically.
offline.connectingHint: The first cold start can take longer. Please wait a moment.
offline.recoveringDescription: OpenClaw Gateway is starting or restarting, then the app will reconnect automatically.
offline.controlUiQueued: Control UI will open automatically when Gateway is ready.
offline.controlUiUnavailable: Control UI cannot be opened yet. Finish Gateway recovery and try again.

notifications.pairedBody: JunQi Desktop successfully paired with Gateway
notifications.title: Notifications

configInvalid.title: Config File Corrupted
configInvalid.hint: The openclaw.json config file contains invalid JSON. This can happen when a multi-agent conversation writes an incorrect config update. The app tried to restore the previous backup automatically. Use Reset Config to remove the broken file and let the gateway start fresh.

configSchemaInvalid.title: Config Values Invalid
configSchemaInvalid.hint: The openclaw.json config file has invalid values, such as an unrecognized binding type or a missing required field. This often happens when a multi-agent conversation writes an incorrect config. The app tried to restore your previous backup automatically. Click Retry to try again, or Reset Config to start fresh.

portInUse.title: Port Already in Use
portInUse.hint: Another process is using the gateway port and could not be stopped automatically. If another app is already using port 18789, close it and click Retry. If the OpenClaw CLI gateway is running, the desktop should connect to it automatically.

timeout.title: Gateway Startup Timed Out
timeout.hint: The gateway process started but did not become ready in time. This can happen after sleep/wake or under heavy load. Click Retry to try again.

notFound.title: Gateway Not Found
notFound.hint: The bundled Node.js or openclaw.mjs could not be located. The installation may be incomplete. Try reinstalling JunQi Desktop.

crash.title: Gateway Crashed on Start
crash.hint: The gateway process exited immediately. Check the logs below for details. A corrupted config file or missing dependency is the most common cause.

unknown.title: Gateway Failed to Start
unknown.hint: An unexpected error occurred. Check the logs below for details.

reset.removed: Config file removed. It will be recreated on next start.

gatewayRescue.title: AI Rescue
gatewayRescue.subtitle: When Gateway cannot start, call the configured model directly to analyze logs and repair paths.
gatewayRescue.noTarget: No directly usable model config was found. You can enter a temporary model for this rescue session; it will not be written to openclaw.json.
gatewayRescue.configReadFailed: Failed to read OpenClaw config. You can still enter a temporary model for this rescue session.
gatewayRescue.readyMessage: A configured model with usable credentials can analyze the startup failure without Gateway.
gatewayRescue.authFailed: {{provider}} rejected the current credential (401). Update its API key or select another diagnostic model.
gatewayRescue.permissionFailed: {{provider}} denied access for the current credential (403). Check model permissions or select another diagnostic model.
gatewayRescue.safetyHint: The API key is used only to authenticate with the selected model service; it is not included in diagnostic text or logs.
gatewayRescue.placeholder: Example: diagnose why Gateway cannot start and give me the safest repair steps.
gatewayRescue.manualIncomplete: Fill in Base URL, API key, and model ID.
gatewayRescue.apiKeyPlaceholder: Temporary API key, not written to config

gatewaySelfRescue.title: Gateway Self-Rescue
gatewaySelfRescue.subtitle: One place for Gateway reconnect, doctor repair, and AI diagnostics.
gatewaySelfRescue.doctorFixHint: Run openclaw doctor --fix to repair OpenClaw environment, config, and runtime issues.
gatewaySelfRescue.hint: Try reconnect/restart first; run Auto Repair if it still fails; use AI Diagnostics when config or logs are unclear.

media.imageControls: Scroll to zoom · Drag to pan · R to rotate · 0 to reset · Esc to close

pairing.enterTokenDesc: Enter the Gateway Token from OpenClaw settings:
pairing.tokenExplanation: JunQi Desktop reads the local OpenClaw configuration first. Enter a token only for a remote Gateway or when no local configuration is available.

channelsCenter.subtitle: Connect agents to Feishu, DingTalk, Telegram, Discord and other message channels.

channelsCenter.noFilterResultsHint: Change the status filter to view other channel accounts.
channelsCenter.emptyHint: Add a channel so agents can receive and respond from messaging apps.

channelsCenter.duplicateAccountId: This account ID already exists in the selected channel.
channelsCenter.gatewayHint: Channel changes require Gateway restart before runtime adapters reconnect.
channelsCenter.gatewayOfflineHint: Gateway is offline. Restart or refresh Gateway to activate this account.

readinessHint.ready: Configuration is complete and an agent is bound.
readinessHint.disabled: This account or channel is disabled and will not receive messages.
readinessHint.missing_credentials: Add the missing credentials before this channel can connect.
readinessHint.unbound: Bind an agent so inbound messages have a target.

channelsCenter.agentHint: Bind each channel account to an agent so inbound messages are routed to the right persona and workspace.
channelsCenter.qrUnavailable: OpenClaw did not return a QR code. Check the Gateway and channel logs.
channelsCenter.qrDenied: The QR sign-in was declined. Generate a new QR code and try again.
channelsCenter.qrTimedOut: The QR code expired. Refresh it and try again.
channelsCenter.qrRenderFailed: The QR code could not be displayed. Refresh it and try again.
channelsCenter.qrRequestFailed: OpenClaw could not start QR login. Check that this OpenClaw version and plugin support it.
channelsCenter.qrVerifying: QR sign-in succeeded. Verifying the channel account with OpenClaw...
channelsCenter.qrNotReady: The scan completed, but OpenClaw could not confirm that the channel account is running. Check channel status and try again.
channelsCenter.qrStatusFailed: The scan completed, but channel status could not be verified. Check the Gateway connection and refresh.
channelsCenter.qrLoginFailed: OpenClaw ended the QR login without connecting this channel. Generate a new QR code and try again.

config.modelRoutingMergeHint: Merge mode can include runtime and built-in provider catalog entries.

config.routingIssueReplaceFallback: One or more fallback models are not declared by an explicit provider.
config.routingIssueRepeatedFallback: A fallback repeats the primary model and will not provide failover.
config.routingIssueUnmatchedPolicy: A session access rule has no match in the configured-only preview.

config.modelSourceProviderHint: Using the model catalog reported by the installed OpenClaw runtime
config.modelSourceSyncedHint: The OpenClaw catalog was unavailable; using the bundled fallback
config.addAuthProfileHint: Add an auth profile for full configuration
config.manageProvidersDesc: Manage providers, models, and API keys
config.codingPlanAdvisory: Coding Plan uses a dedicated API Key and endpoint — do not mix with your general API Key. Get the key from the provider's "Coding Plan" console.
config.localProviderAdvisory: Local and custom endpoints run on your own machine or private server — no cloud API Key needed. vLLM / Custom requires a Base URL; Ollama defaults to localhost:11434.
config.providerIdHint: Written to models.providers[id] in OpenClaw config. Defaults to "custom".
config.baseUrlDriftWarning: Drifted from recommended endpoint ({{url}}) — verify before continuing.
config.modelIdHint: Enter model names, confirm with Enter or comma. OpenClaw will prefix with {{providerId}}/

fileManager.title: File Manager
fileManager.noFilesDesc: No readable files in this folder, or the path is inaccessible.
fileManager.noBridgeDesc: File management requires the Electron IPC bridge (window.aegis). This feature is only available inside JunQi Desktop.
fileManager.uploadsDesc: Manage files you uploaded into managed storage.
fileManager.noOutputFilesDesc: Output file references will appear here when this lane is enabled.
fileManager.noUploadFilesDesc: Files you upload in chat will appear here for preview and export.

storage.title: Choose OpenClaw data location
storage.subtitle: Configuration, sessions, credentials, and workspaces use this location. Node.js, Git, and npm cache follow system defaults.
storage.chooseParent: Choose the folder that will contain OpenClaw data
storage.runtimeRecoverySubtitle: The prior OpenClaw runtime and Gateway service must be restored before data locations can be changed.
storage.migrateHint: Keep configuration, credentials, sessions, and workspaces. The source directory will not be deleted automatically.
storage.startFreshHint: Keep the old directory unchanged and start with an empty configuration in the new location.
storage.installLocationsHint: Workspace, plus optional npm, Node.js, and Git locations
storage.customNpmCacheHint: Off uses npm's default cache for the current operating system and user
storage.npmCacheSettingsHint: Uses npm's system default cache unless an override is saved here.
storage.npmCacheSetupRequired: Complete storage setup before changing the npm download cache.
storage.runtimeSettingsHint: Use system tools by default. Windows uses vendor installers from mainland mirrors first, with Windows Package Manager only as a fallback; a portable runtime is maintained only after you choose a directory.
storage.runtimeSystemManaged: Uses standard operating-system or package-manager locations
storage.runtimeGitSystem: Managed by the macOS/Linux package manager
storage.customNpmPrefixHint: When disabled, use the login terminal npm prefix; choose a location here when it is not writable
storage.customNodeRuntimeHint: Off uses compatible Node.js already installed on the system; on maintains a portable runtime only in the selected directory
storage.customGitRuntimeHint: Off uses the installed system Git; on maintains a portable runtime only in the selected directory
storage.terminalIntegrationHint: Create a dedicated launcher and update the user PATH. This does not modify npmrc or globally replace Node.js or Git. Launcher: {{path}}
storage.migrationLayoutLocked: Migration preserves the workspace and internal runtime layout; Node.js, Git, and npm locations can be selected independently.

autostart.title: Want OpenClaw to start automatically when your computer boots?
autostart.hint: When enabled, OpenClaw quietly runs in the background as soon as your computer starts — your chat channels and scheduled tasks keep working even without opening this app. It's fine to skip: OpenClaw will start whenever you open this app.
autostart.enabledHint: Autostart is on: OpenClaw will run in the background every time your computer boots, no need to open this app. You can turn it off here anytime.
autostart.switching: Switching how OpenClaw runs, one moment…

setup.petReadyHint: You can enter the workspace now.
setup.modeNativeDesc: Install Node.js, Git, and OpenClaw directly on your machine. Best for most users.

setup.pluginUnverifiedStartCheck: The repair of plugin {{plugins}} cannot be verified offline; starting the Gateway to verify…

pluginRecovery.title: A plugin is blocking the Gateway from starting

wizard.title: Configure OpenClaw

wizard.connecting: Connecting to the official OpenClaw setup wizard…
wizard.connectionTimeout: Gateway started, but the setup wizard connection timed out.
wizard.handoffNotReady: OpenClaw setup completed, but the selected Gateway could not be verified after the runtime handoff. Repair it and try again.
wizard.missingStep: The OpenClaw setup wizard did not return its next step.
wizard.sessionExpired: The OpenClaw setup session expired. Reconnect to continue.
wizard.stepSynchronizing: Restoring the OpenClaw setup session. Please wait.
wizard.alreadyRunning: An OpenClaw setup session is still running. Take over setup to restart the selected Gateway and continue without changing the data directory or workspace.
wizard.requestTimeout: The OpenClaw setup request timed out. Reconnect to continue.
wizard.modelNotReady: The selected model has not passed a live check. Continue OpenClaw setup.

channelEnrollment.title: Connect Feishu by QR code
channelEnrollment.subtitle: Scan with your phone and JunQi will continue the official OpenClaw setup.
channelEnrollment.description: Scanning creates and verifies a Feishu app. Credentials are passed only to the official OpenClaw wizard and are never displayed in JunQi.
channelEnrollment.waiting: Scan this code with the Feishu or Lark mobile app.
channelEnrollment.finalizing: Passing the verified credentials to the official OpenClaw wizard…
channelEnrollment.denied: Authorization was declined on the mobile app. Generate a new QR code to try again.
channelEnrollment.expired: This QR code expired. Generate a new one and scan again.
channelEnrollment.networkFailed: Could not connect to Feishu. Check your network and try again.
channelEnrollment.rateLimited: Feishu is busy. Generate a new QR code and try again shortly.
channelEnrollment.providerRejected: Feishu rejected this QR enrollment request. Please try again later.
channelEnrollment.protocolChanged: Feishu changed its QR entrypoint and this JunQi version does not support it yet. Update JunQi and try again.
channelEnrollment.error: The QR service is temporarily unavailable. Generate a new code and try again.
channelEnrollment.handoffFailed: The QR code was verified, but OpenClaw setup could not continue. Generate a new code and try again.
channelEnrollment.handoffRecoverable: The QR code was verified, but the OpenClaw setup session needs to be restored. Select Continue setup; you do not need to scan again.

chat.sessionSyncFailed: Collaboration data could not be synchronized. Last known data may be stale.

card.title: Collaboration run
card.noWorkItems: The work plan is not available yet.

residualExecutionRisk.title: Cancelled locally; OpenClaw Task termination unconfirmed
residualExecutionRisk.summary: JunQi closed this run locally, but the OpenClaw Task may still finish or cause side effects. Keep this audit record.
residualExecutionRisk.details: JunQi cancelled and closed this run locally. OpenClaw Task termination is unconfirmed; external work or side effects may continue. Automatic retention and deletion remain blocked to preserve this audit trail.

actionDialog.verifyUnknownIdentity: Verify the exact OpenClaw Task, Run, and worker session before confirming an outcome.
actionDialog.confirmResidualRisk: I accept the residual risk: the original worker may still finish or produce side effects after this attempt is abandoned.
actionDialog.additionalInputHint: Add facts, constraints, or corrections for the next attempt.
actionDialog.additionalInputPrivacy: This input is bound to exactly the next attempt. It cannot change an active attempt, is stored as internal execution data, and will not be shown here again.
actionDialog.confirmWorkItemCancel: I understand that new dispatch will stop. Any active OpenClaw attempt for this work item will receive a real cancellation request; otherwise the item is cancelled immediately.
actionDialog.noInputEligibleItem: No work item can accept input now. Active attempts must settle or be cancelled first.
actionDialog.noEligibleWorkItem: No work item is eligible for this action.
actionDialog.unknownDelivery: The previous delivery outcome is unknown.
actionDialog.sameDeliveryReconcile: JunQi will reconcile the original delivery with its existing OpenClaw receipt.
actionDialog.flowReconciliationBlockerMessage: Deletion cannot preserve this pending Flow reconciliation. Continuing will permanently abandon it and retain the reason in the audit record.
actionDialog.flowAbandonmentReasonPlaceholder: Record why deleting now is safer than retrying reconciliation.
actionDialog.confirmFlowReconciliationAbandonment: I accept that this unresolved Flow reconciliation will be abandoned and cannot be resumed after deletion.

details.timelineIncompleteMessage: Some audit events are not available in this view. Refresh to confirm the latest authoritative run state.

drawer.title: Collaboration history
drawer.subtitle: Runs remain available across chat sessions.
drawer.emptyHint: Runs created from Chat will appear here with their retained history.
drawer.cleanupPartial: Some managed files still need cleanup.

plugin-missing.title: Collaboration is not installed
plugin-missing.message: Install the JunQi collaboration plugin to run durable multi-agent workflows from Chat.

plugin-not-configured.title: Collaboration needs configuration
plugin-not-configured.message: Choose the coordinator and allowed agents before starting a workflow.

runtime-not-durable.title: Persistent runtime required
runtime-not-durable.message: This Gateway stops with JunQi. Use a system service, persistent Docker runtime, or external Gateway for durable workflows.

version-incompatible.title: Collaboration update required
version-incompatible.message: The installed plugin schema is not compatible with this JunQi version.

no-agents.title: No workflow agents available
no-agents.message: Create or allow at least one OpenClaw agent before starting a collaboration.

error.title: Collaboration is unavailable
error.message: JunQi could not read the collaboration capability. Check the Gateway and try again.

ready.title: Collaboration is ready
ready.message: This runtime can continue workflow execution while JunQi is closed.

bootstrap.title: Collaboration runtime
bootstrap.description: This is only needed for durable multi-agent workflows; normal Chat and Gateway use do not require the collaboration plugin.
bootstrap.loadingBody: JunQi is verifying the target, plugin and recovery journal.
bootstrap.identityBody: Connect to the intended Gateway and wait for its runtime identity to be verified.
bootstrap.identityDescription: Complete Gateway verification before JunQi reads or changes its collaboration plugin.
bootstrap.busyBody: Keep this window open. The durable journal will preserve recovery state if the operation is interrupted.
bootstrap.recoveryBody: An earlier operation did not finish cleanly. Resume it or restore the exact previous plugin state.
bootstrap.healthTitle: Gateway restart and health check pending
bootstrap.healthBody: The fixed package is applied. Restart this Gateway, reconnect, and JunQi will confirm the exact plugin capabilities automatically.
bootstrap.manualBody: JunQi will not change this target. Transfer the fixed archive and run the verified commands on the machine that owns the Gateway.
bootstrap.durableTitle: Move to a persistent runtime first
bootstrap.durableBody: This managed child exits with JunQi. Use a system service or persistent Docker target before enabling collaboration.
bootstrap.unsupportedBody: JunQi could not prove ownership and runtime paths, so mutation remains disabled.
bootstrap.installBody: JunQi will install only the bundled, SHA-256 verified plugin archive shown below.
bootstrap.repairBody: The plugin exists but is disabled or unhealthy. Reapply the fixed bundle to restore its loadable state.
bootstrap.updateBody: The installed version differs from the version bundled with this JunQi release.
bootstrap.readyBody: The expected plugin version is loaded on a persistent runtime.
bootstrap.errorBody: Review the diagnostic below, then verify the Gateway and retry.
bootstrap.noPicker: Package selection is locked to the archive shipped with this JunQi build. Arbitrary files are not accepted by this workflow.
bootstrap.agentConfigPending: Choose one coordinator and an explicit allowed-agent set. JunQi will persist this in the OpenClaw plugin configuration before health can be confirmed.
bootstrap.agentCount: {{count}} OpenClaw agents are available for assignment.
bootstrap.capabilitiesUnavailable: Reconnect or refresh to read the live OpenClaw Agent registry.
bootstrap.noConfigAgents: No OpenClaw Agent is configured on this Gateway.
bootstrap.policyMaintenanceBlocked: Finish the current maintenance operation before changing this policy.
bootstrap.policyRunsBlocked: Finish {{count}} active collaboration run(s) before changing this policy.
bootstrap.rollbackConfirm: I understand that rollback restores the exact previous plugin and configuration state.
bootstrap.restartAvailable: Restart this verified target. Health remains pending until a new Gateway connection advertises the exact durable capabilities.
bootstrap.restartManual: Restart this target with its current supervisor, then reconnect JunQi. Health confirmation will run automatically after the new handshake.
bootstrap.transferArchive: Transfer the exact archive below to the Gateway host.
bootstrap.verifyHash: Verify its SHA-256 equals the value shown above.
bootstrap.runCommands: Run the commands on the target, then reconnect JunQi.

sessionMutation.noAffectedRuns: No active collaboration run is affected.
sessionMutation.fenceActive: An earlier session change is fenced and must be recovered.
sessionMutation.fenceRetained: The collaboration fence remains active. Retry recovery before changing this session.
sessionMutation.proceedDetail: No active collaboration run is bound to this exact session.
sessionMutation.cancelAndWaitDetail: Cancel affected runs, wait for terminal confirmation, then change the session.
sessionMutation.stopAndRetargetDetail: Stop new dispatch, delete the session, and resolve delivery targets from collaboration history.
sessionMutation.recoverDetail: Continue the existing fenced operation instead of starting another one.
